reason: Both answers correctly recommend a Postgres-backed queue, correctly do the scale math (~0.6 jobs/sec), correctly emphasize transactional enqueue as the key differentiator, name appropriate mature libraries, and give sensible revisit thresholds. Neither contains factual errors. Answer A adds a distinct useful caveat (isolating Postgres in a separate instance for spiky/long-running jobs), while Answer B is slightly more concise and gives clearer decision rules for when SQS/Redis might still be preferable (already in AWS, already running Redis) and a nice compact aside on outbox-pattern-equivalence. These are minor, roughly offsetting differences in an otherwise very similar pair of answers — no clear overall winner.
